english: Coconut Shoot Salad (Gỏi Củ Hủ Dừa)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Vietnamese coconut shoot salad; 'salad food product' (FOODON_03311737) is used as the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (composite dishes — salads) is the closest defensible match for a dressed mixed-vegetable salad dish; no specific Vietnamese coconut shoot salad code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Vietnamese coconut shoot salad; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Coconut Shoot Salad is approximately 200 g, presented as a plated salad with dressing, fried shallots, and roasted peanuts on top.

nutrient_source.title: Coconut Shoot Salad (Gỏi Củ Hủ Dừa) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Coconut Shoot Salad (Gỏi Củ Hủ Dừa), cr_id G00010572. All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat is not directly reported by HPB for this entry; it is estimated at approximately 3.5 g/100g based on the known fatty acid profile of fried shallots and peanut oil components, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for fried shallots (FDC ID 170576) and dry-roasted peanuts (FDC ID 172430). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water are set to -1 as HPB does not report these for this dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on mixed vegetable salad with dressing
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese Coconut Shoot Salad (Gỏi Củ Hủ Dừa) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 35 (low) based on the analogous category of mixed non-starchy vegetable salads with fish sauce dressing, which consistently yield GI values in the 30–40 range in the Sydney GI Database. The dish is predominantly fat (44.5 g/100g) and low-carbohydrate (5.9 g/100g), which suppresses postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (35 × 5.9 × 2) / 100 = 4.1 (very low).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100g — well above the 600 mg/100g upper threshold, primarily from fish sauce dressing
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish in its standard preparation; request a reduced-fish-sauce version and avoid adding extra condiments, as a 200 g serving delivers approximately 1620 mg sodium — more than two-thirds of the 2300 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.9 g/100g) and low sugar (4.69 g/100g); estimated GI of 35 (low)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for people with type 2 diabetes in moderate portions due to its low carbohydrate content and low estimated glycemic index; however, the high fat content (44.5 g/100g) contributes significant caloric density, so portion control to approximately 100–150 g is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (44.54 g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~3.5 g/100g), high sodium (810 mg/100g), and cholesterol (26 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high total fat, elevated sodium, and cholesterol makes this dish a high-concern food for cardiovascular disease management; individuals should limit portion size to no more than 100 g, request reduced fish sauce and fried shallots, and avoid frequent consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium (810 mg/100g) worsens tissue swelling; estimated saturated fat exceeds 5 g/100g threshold; chili (nightshade) may trigger joint inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should request chili-free preparation, reduced fish sauce, and fewer fried shallots to lower sodium and saturated fat load, as these components can amplify inflammatory pathways.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coconut Shoot Salad (Gỏi Củ Hủ Dừa) is a traditional Vietnamese salad made from the tender inner shoot of the coconut palm (củ hủ dừa), which is thinly sliced and dressed with a tangy-sweet fish sauce vinaigrette made from fish sauce, lime juice, sugar, and fresh chili. The salad is typically garnished with crispy fried shallots, dry-roasted peanuts, and fresh Vietnamese herbs such as mint and rau răm (Vietnamese coriander). The texture of the coconut shoot is crisp and mildly sweet, similar to palm heart, and it absorbs the dressing well. The dish is served at room temperature as a starter or side dish at Vietnamese meals. Nutritionally, the dish is high in fat (44.5 g/100g) due to the fried shallots and peanut oil used in preparation, and very high in sodium (810 mg/100g) from the fish sauce dressing, making it a high-concern food for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. Carbohydrate content is low at 5.9 g/100g with a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 35, making it suitable for people with type 2 diabetes in controlled portions. The dish is gluten-free and dairy-free but contains fish and peanut allergens. It is commonly found in Vietnamese restaurants in Singapore and is associated with Southern Vietnamese cuisine.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010572, confidence 0.95), which is a primary approved source for Singapore-served dishes. The food identity is exact. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for fried shallots and peanut oil, which is an approved secondary source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for non-starchy vegetable salads.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB for this dish; this is accepted as a confirmed reported value from the approved source, not a zero-macro requiring special justification, as fibre is a secondary nutrient. However, it is noted that coconut shoot typically contains some dietary fibre in raw form; the 0.0 value may reflect the HPB analytical result for the dressed dish as prepared. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and moisture data for this specific Vietnamese coconut shoot salad dish are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for G00010572, and no equivalent entry exists in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1: micronutrient panel data beyond the minerals reported by HPB (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron) are not available in the HPB Foodo database for this dish entry. 4. saturated_fat_g is estimated at 3.5 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central data for fried shallots (FDC ID 170576) and dry-roasted peanuts (FDC ID 172430) proportioned to the dish composition; this is an approved-source estimation and is documented accordingly. 5. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Gỏi Củ Hủ Dừa exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. The input health_suitability entry for 'rheumatoid_arthritis' referenced 'red meat' in its cautions, which is not an ingredient of this dish; this caution has been removed as it is factually incorrect for this food record.
